name: Mile-High Fig Pie (Alternate for Cranberry-Fluff Pie)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour boiling water over figs; let stand 5 minutes. Drain the water and use scissors to snip off the stems, then coarsely chop up the figs.
2. In a saucepan, beat the egg yolks slightly. Stir in the figs, sour cream, lemon juice, salt, and 1/2 cup sugar. Bring to a boil over low heat while stirring, then continue boiling for another 3 minutes. Refrigerate until cool.
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Into the cooled fig mixture, stir in the chopped walnuts, vanilla extract, and grated lemon rind. Pour into the pie shell.
5. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites with salt until frothy. Gradually add 6 tablespoons of sugar, beating well after each addition. Continue beating until stiff peaks form.
6. Spread the beaten mixture evenly over the top of the pie filling and onto the edge of the crust.
7. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until set. Cool.
